user_query:
I'm the president of our local gardening club. Please turn the provided planning meeting notes into clear deliverables I can use with members and volunteers, using a reproducible command-line step.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/meeting_notes.txt
- input/attendees.csv

Requirements:
1) Create a command-line script at tools/garden_meeting_parser.py that:
- Reads input/meeting_notes.txt and input/attendees.csv.
- Parses lines prefixed with DECISION:, EVENT:, and ACTION: from the notes.
  - ACTION format to extract: "ACTION: <Name> to <Task> by <Due date>." Capture assignee_name, task, and due_date.
  - EVENT format to extract: "EVENT: <Title> on <Month DD, YYYY> at <Location>, <Time>." Capture title, date, location, and time when present; if time is not present, leave it empty.
  - If a line begins with "Note:" immediately following an EVENT, attach its text to that event as a notes field.
- Match ACTION assignee_name to attendees.csv by case-insensitive exact name match to get their email. Mark matched_email as true/false and include email if matched.
- Use attendees.csv to list attendees present based on attended=true.
- Write a machine-readable summary to outputs/parsed.json with this top-level structure:
  { "meeting": {"date": "<from header>", "location": "<from header>"}, "attendees_present": ["..."], "decisions": ["..."], "events": [{"title":"...","date":"...","location":"...","time":"...","notes":"..."}], "action_items": [{"assignee_name":"...","task":"...","due_date":"...","matched_email":true/false,"email":"..."}] }
- The meeting date and location should be extracted from the header lines of the notes.

2) Run the script from the command line to generate the following deliverables under outputs/ (create directories as needed):
- outputs/parsed.json (as specified above).
- outputs/meeting_summary.md with clearly labeled sections that include, at minimum:
  - Meeting date and location.
  - Attendees present (from attendees.csv attended=true).
  - Decisions (from DECISION lines).
  - Upcoming events (from EVENT lines), including any attached Note for the corresponding event.
  - Action items split into two lists: matched (those with an email in attendees.csv) and unmatched (no email found in attendees.csv).
  - Counts: number of decisions, events, action items; plus matched vs. unmatched action item counts.
- outputs/member_update_email.txt containing a plain-text email to all members with:
  - A first line starting with "Subject: " that includes the meeting date.
  - A body that summarizes key decisions and lists each upcoming event with at least its title and date; include the next meeting line from the notes if present.
- For each matched assignee (i.e., with an email in attendees.csv), create an individualized volunteer message file at outputs/volunteer_emails/<AssigneeName>.txt containing:
  - A "To: <email>" line using the email from attendees.csv.
  - A "Subject: " line that references action items from the meeting.
  - A body listing all tasks and due dates assigned to that person.

Rules and constraints:
- Do not hardcode names or emails; derive them from input files.
- Name matching is case-insensitive and must be exact on the full name.
- Unmatched action items (assignees not in attendees.csv) must be listed under the unmatched section and should not produce a volunteer email.
- Keep dates and times as they appear in the notes (no reformatting required).
- The script must be runnable from the command line, e.g.: python tools/garden_meeting_parser.py --notes input/meeting_notes.txt --attendees input/attendees.csv --outdir outputs
- Do not modify the input files or fetch anything from the Internet.

Deliverables to produce in the workspace:
- tools/garden_meeting_parser.py
- outputs/parsed.json
- outputs/meeting_summary.md
- outputs/member_update_email.txt
- outputs/volunteer_emails/<one file per matched assignee>.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
